issues.section: 4. Key Findings — Key Drivers of Purchase Intent — Figure 5
issues.description: The SVG axis label for Figure 5 (Key Drivers of Purchase Intent — Derived Importance chart) is incorrect. The x-axis has gridlines at x=200 (labeled '0.0') and x=312 (labeled '0.1'). This establishes a scale of 112 pixels per 0.1 units. By that scale, x=424 corresponds to 0.2 (not 0.3). The axis label at x=424 reads '0.3', which is wrong. Note also that bar widths are not precisely to scale relative to this axis (e.g., quality bar width=350 would map to r=0.3125 on the axis, not 0.4379), suggesting the chart is diagrammatic with an approximate axis. Regardless, the axis label '0.3' is factually inconsistent with the scale markers shown.
issues.claimed_value: '0.3' (axis label text at x=424 in Figure 5 SVG)
issues.correct_value: '0.2' (based on scale established by 0.0 at x=200 and 0.1 at x=312, giving 112px per 0.1 unit; x=424 = 0.0 + 2*0.1 = 0.2)
issues.source_file: derived_importance.csv (provides the actual r values the axis is meant to represent)
issues.fix_instruction: In the SVG for Figure 5 (Key Drivers of Purchase Intent chart), locate the axis label text element at x=424, y=418 and change its content from '0.3' to '0.2'. The corrected line should read: <text x="424" y="418" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#94A3B8" font-family="Consolas,monospace">0.2</text>. Optionally, add a fourth marker at x=536 labeled '0.4' to better represent the quality attribute (r=0.4379) which extends beyond the current rightmost axis label.

verified_stats: All primary statistics in the report were verified against source CSVs and are accurate. Specific items confirmed correct: (1) Sample sizes: 800 total, 400 per wave, 100% retention rate, 46 non-responses on Q11 (5.75%), valid Q11 N=754 — all match cleaning_summary.json. (2) Aided awareness: 2025=261/400=65.25%, 2026=330/400=82.5% (+17.25pp, chi2=29.95, p<0.001) — confirmed from banner_year_Q3_aided_Acme_Strawberries.csv and significance_results.csv. (3) Combined aided awareness: 591/800=73.88%, reported as 73.9% — acceptable rounding. (4) Unaided awareness: 2025=123/400=30.75%, 2026=167/400=41.75% (+11pp, chi2=10.00, p=0.002) — confirmed. (5) Acme purchase rate: 2025=114/400=28.5%, 2026=168/400=42.0% (+13.5pp, chi2=15.38, p<0.001) — confirmed. (6) Brand preference Acme: 2025=59/370=15.95%, 2026=132/384=34.375% reported as 34.38% — confirmed from banner_year_Q11_brand_preference.csv. (7) Brand preference Crimson Crown: 2025=114/370=30.81%, 2026=92/384=23.96% — confirmed. (8) Q11 combined waves: Crimson Crown=206/754=27.32% reported as 27.3%, Acme=191/754=25.33% reported as 25.3%, Sunrise=115/754=15.25% reported as 15.3%, Red Valley=93/754=12.33% reported as 12.3%, FreshPick=77/754=10.21% reported as 10.2%, Golden Meadow=50/754=6.63% reported as 6.6%, Harvest Hill=22/754=2.92% reported as 2.9% — all rounding acceptable. (9) Q6 purchase intent combined: score 3=466/800=58.25% ✓, score 4=223 (27.88% reported as 27.9%) ✓, score 5=7 (0.875% reported as 0.9%) ✓, score 1=1 (0.125% reported as 0.1%) ✓. (10) Q6 by wave: 2025 positive=67/400=16.75%, 2026 positive=163/400=40.75%; 2025 neutral=253/400=63.25%, 2026 neutral=213/400=53.25%; 2025 negative=80/400=20.0%, 2026 negative=24/400=6.0% — confirmed from banner_year_Q6_purchase_intent.csv. (11) NPS: promoters (9+10)=149/800=18.625% reported as 18.6% ✓; detractors (0-6)=430/800=53.75% reported as 53.8% ✓; NPS=-35 ✓. NPS score 10: 12 from 2025, 54 from 2026, 54/66=81.82% reported as 81.8% ✓. NPS score 2: 27 from 2025, 27/34=79.41% reported as 79.4% ✓. Q10 chi2=130.976 reported as 130.98 ✓. (12) Q5_01 quality: 2025 top-2-box=(86+17)/400=103/400=25.75% ✓; 2026=(140+70)/400=210/400=52.5% ✓; score 5 only: 2025=17/400=4.25% ✓, 2026=70/400=17.5% ✓; combined top-2-box=(226+87)/800=313/800=39.125% reported as 39.1% ✓; combined score 3=330/800=41.25% ✓; chi2=76.61 ✓. (13) Q5_02 freshness: 2025 top-2-box=(103+14)/400=117/400=29.25% ✓; 2026=(154+47)/400=201/400=50.25% ✓; combined top-2-box=(257+61)/800=318/800=39.75% ✓; score 3=319/800=39.875% reported as 39.9% ✓; chi2=45.95 ✓. (14) Q5_10 trust: 2025 top-2-box=(74+11)/400=85/400=21.25% ✓; 2026=(93+31)/400=124/400=31.0% ✓; combined top-2-box=(167+42)/800=209/800=26.125% reported as 26.1% ✓; score 3=391/800=48.875% reported as 48.9% ✓; chi2=26.50 ✓. (15) Q5_06 consistency: 2025 top-2-box=(66+4)/400=70/400=17.5% ✓; 2026=(120+29)/400=149/400=37.25% ✓; chi2=52.62 ✓. (16) Q8 Acme frequency: weekly 2025=12/400=3.0% ✓, 2026=39/400=9.75% ✓; often+weekly 2025=39/400=9.75% ✓, 2026=90/400=22.5% ✓; chi2=29.66 ✓. Combined weekly=51/800=6.375% reported as 6.4% ✓. (17) Crimson Crown purchase rate: 2025=195/400=48.75% ✓, 2026=143/400=35.75% ✓; chi2=13.33 ✓. (18) Category frequency p=0.143 (not significant) — source shows p=0.142958 ✓. (19) Income distribution: $100K+=297 (297/800=37.125% reported as 37.1% ✓), $50K-$99K=310 (310/800=38.75% reported as 38.8% ✓), Under $50K=193 (193/800=24.125% reported as 24.1% ✓) — confirmed from banner_Q17_income column totals. (20) Income quality finding: $100K+ score5=22/297=7.407% reported as 7.4% ✓; $50K-$99K=40/310=12.903% reported as 12.9% ✓; Under$50K=25/193=12.953% reported as 13.0% ✓; chi2=18.85, p=0.016, min expected=3.14 ✓. (21) Income category frequency chi2=17.54, p=0.025 ✓. (22) Gender shelf life chi2=16.66, p=0.034, min expected=0.73 ✓. (23) Age Harvest Hill chi2=6.38 (source=6.3761) ✓; age sustainability chi2=16.26 (source=16.2589) ✓. (24) Significance test counts: wave=21 ✓ (confirmed by counting significance_results.csv rows with demographic_col='year' and significant_05=True), age=2 ✓, gender=1 ✓, region=0 ✓, income=2 ✓, total=26 ✓. Table 10 and all prose text correctly state 21 wave significant tests. (25) All 11 derived importance Pearson r and standardized beta values match derived_importance.csv exactly. (26) Q6 chi2=76.32 (source=76.3226) ✓. (27) Q11 chi2=34.37 (source=34.3727) ✓. (28) Q9 chi2=32.32 (source=32.3188) ✓. (29) Q7 age p-values for borderline results and all other demographic p-values verified against significance_results.csv.
